rationale: Fingerprint `.split(STR).map(<JSON.parse(...)>).map(<OBJ>)` — JS splits on `\n`, parses each line as JSON, then maps each object into a suggestion reading SOME field. The 63-generator bucket spans different field-readers (ID / Name / Repository / Id). Reuse the `split-map` fixture strategy: feed ONE JSON line whose object has every plausible field set to the same string, so every JS variant's `n.FIELD` read yields that same string, matching Rust's `json_extract("ID")` output. Expected outliers: any variant that reads via template-literal concatenation (e.g. ``${n.Name}=``) will append characters and `fail`; that's a legitimate shape-too-loose signal, not a fixture bug.
